description: Installer and launcher polish: fix the .desktop Exec and Icon paths to be absolute or generate the desktop entry with a script; improve launch.bat and setup_desktop_app.bat to detect existing venvs, handle upgrades, and provide clearer error messages.
steps: Create scripts/install_desktop_entry.sh that: (1) resolves the repo root to an absolute path, (2) generates a .desktop file with absolute Exec and Icon paths pointing into the repo, (3) copies it to ~/.local/share/applications/, (4) prints success message

Update SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop to use placeholders that the script replaces, or make the script generate it fresh. The Exec should be: bash -c 'cd "/absolute/path" && exec ./launch.sh' and Icon should be /absolute/path/src/stx/gui/assets/logo.png

In launch.bat: (1) detect if .venv exists but stx-app.exe is missing (upgrade scenario) and re-run pip install, (2) add a check for Python version >= 3.9, (3) improve error messages to be more specific about what went wrong
In setup_desktop_app.bat: (1) detect if .venv already exists and offer to upgrade (re-run pip install) rather than recreating, (2) add a Python version check, (3) improve error messages
Update the comments in SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop to reference the new install script instead of manual sed commands
acceptance_criteria: scripts/install_desktop_entry.sh generates a valid .desktop file with absolute paths
launch.bat handles the upgrade scenario (venv exists, app missing) gracefully
setup_desktop_app.bat detects existing venvs and upgrades cleanly
SalesforceTranslationHandler.desktop comments point to the install script
All launcher scripts provide actionable error messages on failure
verification: bash scripts/install_desktop_entry.sh (should produce a valid .desktop file)
findings: All launcher scripts updated successfully. install_desktop_entry.sh verified to produce valid .desktop file with absolute paths. All 74 tests pass. Cannot test .bat files on Linux but logic is straightforward.
